data.content:
Lipodistrofia
Lipodistrofia is a change in the skin tissue.<SEP>Lipodistrofia is a condition that occurs when there is a change in the skin tissue where insulin is applied frequently.

Agulha
Agulha is a type of needle used for insulin injection, requiring proper handling and technique for safe and effective use.<SEP>Agulha is a needle used for injecting insulin into the body.

Diabetes tipo 1
Diabetes tipo 1 is a condition where the body is unable to produce insulin due to an autoimmune response.

Lipo-hipertrofia
Lipo-hipertrofia is a type of lipodistrofia where the skin becomes hardened and nodular due to the accumulation of fat in the area of insulin application.

Rodízio
Rodízio is a method of rotating the injection sites to prevent lipodistrofia.

Diabetes tipo 2
Diabetes tipo 2 is a condition where the body is resistant to insulin and is unable to use it effectively.

Lipoatrofia
Lipoatrofia is a rare type of lipodistrofia where there is a loss of fat in the area, forming depressions or holes in the skin.

Profissional de saúde
Profissional de saúde is a healthcare professional who provides guidance and care for individuals with diabetes.

Hormônio
Hormônio is a chemical that regulates various bodily functions.

Glicose
Glicose refers to blood sugar levels that need to be regulated.<SEP>Glicose is a type of sugar that is essential for the body's energy production.<SEP>Glicose is a type of sugar present in the body's interstitial fluid.

Células do pâncreas
Células do pâncreas are specialized cells in the pancreas that produce insulin.

Sistema de defesa
Sistema de defesa is the body's immune system that responds to threats and infections.

Pâncreas
The Pâncreas is an organ responsible for producing insulin and other hormones that regulate digestion and metabolism.<SEP>Pâncreas is an organ that produces hormones and enzymes necessary for digestion and energy production.

Disglicemia
Disglicemia is a condition where the blood glucose levels are not balanced, resulting in frequent fluctuations.

Memória metabólica
Memória metabólica is a system where the body "remembers" the effects of high blood glucose levels, leading to long-term complications.

Hemoglobina Glicada (HbA1c)
Hemoglobina glicada (HbA1c) é um exame que mostra a média da glicose nos últimos 3 meses.<SEP>Hemoglobin A1c is a measure of average blood glucose control over the past 2-3 months.<SEP>Hemoglobina Glicada (HbA1c) is a type of substance related to blood glucose levels.

Glicemia em Jejum
Fasting blood glucose is a measure of blood glucose levels after an overnight fast.

Grupo de Pacientes Hemoglobina Glicada
Grupo de Pacientes Hemoglobina Glicada is a group of patients with hemoglobin A1c, providing guidelines for blood glucose control.

Tempo no Alvo (70–180 mg/dL)
The target range for blood glucose is between 70 and 180 mg/dL.

Glicemia após as Refeições (2h)
Postprandial blood glucose is a measure of blood glucose levels 2 hours after a meal.

Hipoglicemia (<70 mg/dL)
Hypoglycemia is a blood glucose level below 70 mg/dL.

Adultos em geral (DM1 ou DM2)
Adults with diabetes type 1 or 2 are advised to follow specific blood glucose targets.

Crianças e adolescentes (DM1)
Children and adolescents with diabetes type 1 are advised to follow specific blood glucose targets.

Idosos saudáveis
Healthy elderly individuals are advised to follow specific blood glucose targets.

Federal Reserve Policy Announcement
The Federal Reserve's policy announcement is expected to impact investor confidence and market stability.

Idosos com doenças ou limitações
Elderly individuals with diseases or limitations are advised to follow specific blood glucose targets.

Mulheres grávidas com diabetes
Women with diabetes during pregnancy are advised to follow specific blood glucose targets.

Diabetes gestacional
Diabetes gestacional refers to diabetes that develops during pregnancy.<SEP>Diabetes gestacional is a condition where women develop diabetes only during pregnancy, requiring careful management to protect both mother and baby.

Sensor de glicose (CGM)
CGM is a device that measures glucose levels continuously.<SEP>Glucose sensors (CGM) are used to monitor blood glucose levels during pregnancy, helping women achieve better glycemic control.

Gordura
Gordura is a type of tissue where insulin is absorbed, requiring proper injection technique to ensure effective absorption.<SEP>Gordura is the recommended site for insulin injection, due to its numerous small blood vessels.

data.entity_name: Onde aplicar a insulina e como ela age
data.content:
Onde aplicar a insulina e como ela age
Onde aplicar a insulina e como ela age is a topic discussing the correct application and action of insulin.

Tokyo
Tokyo is the host city of the World Athletics Championship.<SEP>Tokyo is the location where the World Athletics Championship took place.<SEP>Tokyo não é mencionado como uma localização relevante no texto.

Omega Energy
Omega Energy is an energy company that gained 2.1% in stock value due to rising oil prices.

Global Tech Index
The Global Tech Index tracks the performance of major technology stocks and experienced a 3.4% decline today.

Noah Carter
Noah Carter is a sprinter who broke the 100m sprint record using cutting-edge carbon-fiber spikes.<SEP>Noah Carter is a person who participated in the World Athletics Championship.<SEP>Noah Carter não é mencionado como um personagem relevante no texto.

Nexon Technologies
Nexon Technologies is a tech company that saw its stock decline by 7.8% after disappointing earnings.

Elliott P. Joslin
Elliott P. Joslin was a doctor who helped train nurses to educate people with diabetes on proper insulin use.

World Athletics Championship
The World Athletics Championship is a global sports competition featuring top athletes in track and field.<SEP>The World Athletics Championship is an event that is not directly related to the input text, but is mentioned as a separate entity.<SEP>World Athletics Championship is an international athletic competition.<SEP>World Athletics Championship não é mencionado como um evento relevante no texto.

Canetas recarregáveis de insulina
Canetas recarregáveis de insulina are reusable insulin pens that help with insulin administration.

Educação em diabetes
Educação em diabetes refers to the education and training of people with diabetes on proper insulin use and management.

Paciente bem informado
Paciente bem informado is a term used by doctor Elliott P. Joslin to emphasize the importance of educating patients on proper insulin use.

Insulina em suspensão
Insulina em suspensão is a type of insulin that requires shaking before use.

i-Port
i-Port is a device that allows for multiple insulin injections without needing to change the needle daily.

Seringa
A seringa é um equipamento utilizado para aplicar insulina de forma segura e precisa.<SEP>Seringa is a device used to inject insulin into the body.<SEP>Seringa is a type of syringe used for insulin injection, requiring proper handling and technique for safe and effective use.

Caneta
Caneta é um equipamento utilizado para aplicar insulina de forma segura e precisa, especialmente em pacientes com diabetes.

Álcool
Álcool é um produto utilizado para limpar e desinfetar equipamentos e superfícies.

Algodo
Algodo é um material utilizado para aplicar compressão no local da injeção de insulina.

Frasco
Frasco é um equipamento utilizado para armazenar insulina em forma líquida.<SEP>Frasco is a container used to store insulin.

Posto de Saúde
Posto de Saúde é uma organização que fornece cuidados de saúde e serviços de saúde para a população.

Geladeira
Geladeira é um equipamento utilizado para armazenar insulina em temperatura controlada.

Álcool 70%
Álcool 70% is a disinfectant used to clean canetas and skin.<SEP>Álcool 70% é um produto utilizado para limpar e desinfetar equipamentos e superfícies.

Unidade Básica de Saúde (UBS)
The Unidade Básica de Saúde (UBS) is a healthcare unit.

Farmácias
Pharmacies are establishments that sell medications.

Agulhas
Needles are medical devices used to inject medications or fluids into the body.

Lancetas (agulhinhas)
Lancetas, or agulhinhas, are small needles used for testing blood glucose.

Seringas
Syringes are medical devices used to inject medications or fluids into the body.

Casas Cirúrgicas
Surgical houses are establishments that sell medical supplies.

Canetas de insulina
Insulin pens are medical devices used to inject insulin into the body.

Bombas de insulina
Insulin pumps are medical devices used to administer insulin to the body.

Protocolos
Protocols are standardized procedures for healthcare providers to follow.

Receitas médicas
Medical prescriptions are written orders from healthcare providers for medications or treatments.

Prescrição eletrônica
Electronic prescription is a system for healthcare providers to write and send prescriptions electronically.

Pele
The skin is the outer covering of the body, used as the site for insulin application.

Coxas
The thighs are a suitable site for insulin application, specifically the front and lateral external area.

Braços
The arms are a suitable site for insulin application, specifically the area between four fingers below the armpit and four fingers above the elbow.

Nádegas
The buttocks are a suitable site for insulin application, specifically the upper lateral external quadrant.

Abdômen
The abdomen is a suitable site for insulin application, specifically the lateral right and left areas, maintaining a distance of three to four fingers from the navel.<SEP>Abdômen is a region of the body where insulin can be injected, but is not the preferred site for insulin absorption, especially for pregnant women.

Gestante
A pregnant woman is a specific population that requires special considerations for insulin application.

Bebê
The baby is a vulnerable individual whose well-being must be prioritized during insulin application.<SEP>The baby is affected by the mother's diabetes during pregnancy, with potential risks including being born large or requiring neonatal urinary tract infections (UTIs).

Caroços
Lipohypertrophy is a condition characterized by the formation of nodules or lumps in the skin, often as a result of repeated insulin injections in the same area.<SEP>Caroços are small, localized areas of fat accumulation that can occur at injection sites, leading to insulin resistance and other complications.

data.entity_name: Rodízio dos locais de aplicação da insulina
data.content:
Rodízio dos locais de aplicação da insulina
The rotation of injection sites is an essential aspect of insulin therapy, helping to prevent lipohypertrophy and maintain optimal blood sugar control.

Lipohipertrofia
Lipohipertrofia refers to the formation of lumps or nodules in the skin due to repeated insulin injections.<SEP>Lipohypertrophy is a condition characterized by the formation of nodules or lumps in the skin, often as a result of repeated insulin injections in the same area.<SEP>Lipohipertrofia is a condition characterized by the accumulation of fat in localized areas, which can lead to insulin resistance and other complications.

Cuidador
A caregiver is an individual who assists with insulin application, providing support and guidance.

data.entity_name: Mulheres que usam sensor de glicose (CGM) durante a gravidez
data.content:
Mulheres que usam sensor de glicose (CGM) durante a gravidez
Women who use glucose sensors during pregnancy have better glycemic control and fewer complications.

Mãe
The mother plays a crucial role in managing diabetes during pregnancy to protect her baby.

Glicemia
Glicemia refers to the level of glucose in the blood, which is regulated by insulin.<SEP>Glycemia refers to blood glucose levels, which must be carefully managed during pregnancy to prevent complications.

ACOG (EUA)
ACOG (EUA) is an association of medical professionals that provides guidelines for managing diabetes during pregnancy.

CDA (Canadá)
CDA (Canadá) is an association of medical professionals that provides guidelines for managing diabetes during pregnancy.

UTI neonatal
Neonatal urinary tract infections (UTIs) are a potential complication of diabetes during pregnancy.

ADA (EUA)
ADA (EUA) is an association of medical professionals that provides guidelines for managing diabetes during pregnancy.

IDF (Internacional)
IDF (Internacional) is an association of medical professionals that provides guidelines for managing diabetes during pregnancy.

NICE (Reino Unido)
NICE (Reino Unido) is an association of medical professionals that provides guidelines for managing diabetes during pregnancy.

Diabetes
Diabetes is a chronic condition that affects blood sugar regulation, requiring proper management and treatment, including insulin application and rodizio.<SEP>Diabetes is a chronic condition where the body is unable to regulate blood sugar levels effectively.<SEP>Diabetes is a medical condition characterized by high blood sugar levels, which can be further divided into type 1 and type 2 diabetes.

Refeição
Refeição refers to a meal, often used in the context of managing blood glucose levels during pregnancy.

neos
neos is a character in the input text.

Jejum
Jejum é um estado de alimentação que é comum em pessoas com diabetes.<SEP>Jejum refers to the state of fasting or not having eaten for a certain period, often used in medical contexts to measure blood glucose levels.

MG/dL
MG/dL is a unit of measurement for blood glucose levels, with higher values indicating higher glucose concentrations.

Podem
Podem refers to a group of people who can change the form of some cells, including the defense system.

médicos
médicos are medical professionals who contribute to the concept of "memória metabólica" or the body's ability to store the effects of high sugar levels.

açúcar
açúcar is a product that is high in sugar, which can have negative effects on the body.

insulina
insulina is a product that is used to treat diabetes.

cães
cães are creatures that were used in the discovery of insulin.

diabetes
diabetes is a condition that affects the body's ability to regulate blood sugar levels.

tratamento
tratamento is an event or a treatment for diabetes.

urina
urina is a product that contains sugar and was used in early tests for diabetes.

memória metabólica
memória metabólica is a concept that refers to the body's ability to store the effects of high sugar levels.

dedo
dedo is a body part that is used to collect a drop of blood for testing.

sangue
sangue is a product that contains insulin and is used to treat diabetes.

furinho
furinho is a body part that is used to collect a drop of blood for testing.

pele
pele is a body part that is used to attach a sensor to track blood sugar levels.

sensor
sensor is a device that measures blood sugar levels and is used to track the levels over time.

glicosímetro
glicosímetro is a device that measures blood sugar levels.

HbA1c
HbA1c is a product that measures the average blood sugar levels over a period of time.

Federal Reserve
The Federal Reserve is an organization that is not directly related to the input text, but is mentioned as a potential influence on the market.

Associação Americana de Diabetes
The American Diabetes Association is a global organization that provides recommendations for diabetes management, including insulin application and rodizio.

Caneta de Insulina
Caneta de Insulina is a type of insulin pen used for insulin injection, requiring proper handling and technique for safe and effective use.

Rodizio
Rodizio is a technique used to rotate the injection site for insulin application, helping to prevent skin irritation and improve insulin absorption.

Músculo
Músculo is a type of tissue where insulin can be injected, but is not the preferred site for insulin absorption.

Via Subcutânea
Via Subcutânea is the preferred method for insulin injection, where insulin is absorbed into the bloodstream through the subcutaneous tissue.

Diabetes Mellitus
Diabetes Mellitus is a chronic condition that affects blood sugar regulation, requiring proper management and treatment, including insulin application and rodizio.

Hospital
Hospital is a medical facility where insulin can be administered intravenously or intramuscularly, but is not the preferred site for insulin absorption.

Insuloterapia
Insuloterapia is a treatment approach that involves the use of insulin to control blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes.

Diabetes Tipo 1
Diabetes Tipo 1 is a condition where the body does not produce insulin, requiring the use of insulin therapy.

Diabetes Tipo 2
Diabetes Tipo 2 is a condition where the body either does not produce enough insulin or cannot effectively use the insulin it produces.

Açúcar
Açúcar refers to glucose, a type of sugar that is a primary source of energy for the body.

Remédios Orais
Remédios Orais refers to oral medications, which may not be sufficient to control blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes.

Basal-Bolus
Basal-Bolus is a treatment approach that combines long-acting insulin (basal) with rapid-acting insulin (bolus) to control blood sugar levels.

Bomba de Insulina
Bomba de Insulina is a device that delivers small doses of insulin throughout the day.

Diabetes Gestacional
Diabetes Gestacional is a condition that occurs during pregnancy, where the mother's body does not produce enough insulin to meet the demands of the growing fetus.

Pós-Operatório
Pós-Operatório is a condition that occurs after surgery, where the body may experience insulin resistance and glucose metabolism changes.

Febre
Febre is a condition characterized by an elevated body temperature, which can affect insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism.

Paciente
Paciente refers to the individual receiving insulin treatment.<SEP>Paciente is an individual with diabetes who requires treatment and monitoring.

Profissional de Saúde
Profissional de Saúde refers to a healthcare professional who prescribes and monitors insulin therapy.

Canetas
Canetas are devices used to administer insulin to patients.

Infecção
Infecção is a condition characterized by the invasion of the body by pathogens, which can lead to inflammation and insulin resistance.

Federal University of Health
Federal University of Health is an organization that provides health services and guidelines.

Tamanho da agulha
Tamanho da agulha refers to the length of the insulin needle used.

Depósito rígido
Depósito rígido refers to the rigid container used to store used needles and lancets.

Unidade de Saúde
Unidade de Saúde refers to the health unit where patients can dispose of used needles and lancets.

Prega Subcutânea
Prega Subcutânea refers to the subcutaneous injection technique used to administer insulin.

Passo a passo
Passo a passo refers to the step-by-step instructions for administering insulin.

Crianças
Crianças refers to children who require insulin treatment.

Adolescentes
Adolescentes refers to teenagers who require insulin treatment.

Sensor em tempo real
Sensor em tempo real (tR-SMCG) is a device that measures blood glucose levels in real-time.

Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM)
Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) is a technology that tracks blood glucose levels over a 24-hour period.

Eversense
Eversense is a continuous glucose monitoring system developed by Senseonics.

Intersticial
Intersticial is a type of fluid present in the body that contains glicose.

Dexcom
Dexcom is a company that develops and manufactures continuous glucose monitoring systems.

FreeStyle Libre
FreeStyle Libre is a continuous glucose monitoring system developed by Abbott.

Bomba de insulina
Bomba de insulina is an insulin pump used to deliver insulin to the body.

Enlite
Enlite is a continuous glucose monitoring system used with insulin pumps.

Medtronic MiniMed
Medtronic MiniMed is an insulin pump developed by Medtronic.

Glicosímetro
Glicosímetro is a device used to measure blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes.<SEP>Glicosímetro is a device used to measure blood glucose levels.

Sensor de Glicose
Sensor de Glicose is a device used to continuously monitor blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes.<SEP>Sensor de glicose facilita o controle do diabetes, evita crises de hipoglicemia ou hiperglicemia e ajuda a ajustar o tratamento.

Médico
Médico is a healthcare professional who provides guidance and treatment to individuals with diabetes.

Intersticial Fluid
Intersticial Fluid is a type of fluid present in the body that contains glicose.

Caderninho
Caderninho is a notebook used to record and track blood sugar levels and medication.

DM1
DM1 é uma forma de diabetes.

Hipoglicemia
Hipoglicemia is a condition where the blood sugar level is lower than normal.<SEP>Hipoglicemia é um estado de baixa glicose no sangue.

Aparelho
Aparelho is a device used to store and display blood sugar levels and medication information.

Glicemia Capilar
Glicemia capilar é um exame que mostra a glicose no exato momento.

Aplicativo de Celular
Aplicativo de Celular is a mobile application used to track and monitor blood sugar levels.

Federal Medical Organization
Federal Medical Organization é uma entidade que regula a prática médica.

DM2
DM2 é uma forma de diabetes.

Hiperglicemia
Hiperglicemia é um estado de alta glicose no sangue.

Insulina
Insulina is a hormone used to regulate blood sugar levels in patients, particularly those with diabetes. It is produced by the pancreas and plays a crucial role in facilitating the entry of glucose into cells, thereby helping the body to absorb glucose from the blood and convert it into energy.

Insulina is administered via injection or pump, and its application requires proper technique to be effective. The medication is used to control blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes, and its use is often necessary for patients who struggle to manage their condition through diet and exercise alone.

In addition to its role in regulating blood sugar levels, Insulina also helps to prevent the complications associated with diabetes, such as kidney damage and nerve damage. By controlling blood sugar levels, Insulina helps to reduce the risk of these complications and improve the overall health and well-being of patients with diabetes.

Insulina is a hormone that is essential for the proper functioning of the body's metabolic processes. It works by facilitating the entry of glucose into cells, which helps to regulate blood sugar levels and prevent the complications associated with diabetes. Insulina is a medication that is used to regulate blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes, and its use is often necessary for patients who struggle to manage their condition through diet and exercise alone.

Complicações
Complicações are the long-term consequences of frequent fluctuations in blood glucose levels, which can be prevented by maintaining a balanced blood glucose level.

Inflamações internas
Inflamações internas are a type of complication that can arise from frequent fluctuations in blood glucose levels, which can lead to the development of diabetes-related complications.

Vasos sanguíneos
Vasos sanguíneos are the blood vessels that can be damaged by frequent fluctuations in blood glucose levels, leading to the development of vascular complications.

Vez
Insulina is absorbed very quickly when injected into the veia.

Tempo de absorção
Insulina is absorbed more quickly when applied to the braços, coxas, and nádegas.

Exercício
Exercício can cause Insulina to act more quickly and cause hypoglycemia.

Calor
Calor can cause Insulina to act more quickly and cause hypoglycemia.

Fevereira
Fevereira can cause Insulina to act more quickly and cause hypoglycemia.

Banho quente
Banho quente can cause Insulina to act more quickly and cause hypoglycemia.

Compressa quente
Compressa quente can cause Insulina to act more quickly and cause hypoglycemia.

Aprender e praticar
Aprender e praticar is essential for proper insulin use, as discussed in Onde aplicar a insulina e como ela age.

Massagem
Massagem can cause Insulina to act more quickly and cause hypoglycemia.

Compressa fria
Compressa fria can cause Insulina to act more slowly and cause hyperglycemia.

Banho frio
Banho frio can cause Insulina to act more slowly and cause hyperglycemia.

Desidratação
Desidratação can cause Insulina to act more slowly and cause hyperglycemia.

Dexcom G6
Dexcom G6 is a type of continuous glucose monitoring system used with Sensor em tempo real (tR-SMCG).

CGM
Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) is a technology that tracks blood glucose levels over a 24-hour period using sensors like Sensor em tempo real (tR-SMCG).
